headlines.headline: US Disables Ship in Gulf During Iran Blockade
headlines.summary: The US military struck a commercial ship attempting to breach the blockade and reach Iran with a Hellfire missile. The incident highlights continued tensions in the Gulf despite ongoing ceasefire negotiations between Washington and Tehran.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Drones Strike Russian Oil Facility
headlines.summary: Ukrainian drones attacked a Russian oil facility as Kyiv and Moscow traded drone attacks. More than 13,000 residents in Zaporizhzhia were left without power as Russia and Ukraine exchanged strikes.

headlines.headline: Hegseth Addresses Shangri-La Dialogue on US Foreign Policy
headlines.summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth outlined Americas geopolitical goals at the Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore, addressing defense leaders amid questions about US commitment to Asian allies and ongoing global conflicts.

headlines.headline: Macron Faces Backlash After Interrupting Africa Summit Panel
headlines.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron faced criticism after he stormed the stage at the Africa Forward Summit in Kenya to demand silence from the audience. The incident comes as France attempts to reshape its relationship with African nations as a partnership of equals rather than a former colonial power.

headlines.headline: Russian Drone Hits Romanian Apartment Block
headlines.summary: A Russian drone struck a block of flats in Romania near the Ukrainian border, causing a fire and injuring two people. Residents expressed fear as Ukraine-related attacks spill over into NATO territory.

headlines.headline: Russia Recalls Ambassador to Armenia Over EU Rapprochement
headlines.summary: Moscow recalled its envoy to Yerevan for consultations in protest of Armenia's deepening ties with the European Union ahead of a June 7 election, signaling growing tensions in Russia's traditional sphere of influence.

headlines.headline: Myanmar Junta Chief Makes First Foreign Visit to India
headlines.summary: Min Aung Hlaing, who recently transitioned from junta chief to president, is visiting India in his first overseas trip since taking the civilian role, seeking regional legitimacy while continuing a brutal civil war.

perspectives.angle: Myanmars Min Aung Hlaing Makes First Foreign Visit as Leader to India
perspectives.summary: Myanmar junta chief Min Aung Hlaing embarked on his first foreign tour since seizing power, visiting India in a significant diplomatic move as the military government seeks international legitimacy.

headlines.headline: Israel Added to UN Sexual Violence Blacklist for First Time
headlines.summary: The United Nations has added Israel to its blacklist of parties responsible for sexual violence in conflict zones, marking an unprecedented diplomatic blow for the country amid ongoing international scrutiny of its military operations.

perspectives.angle: Israel Added to UN Sexual Violence in Conflict Blacklist for First Time
perspectives.summary: The UN Secretary-General added Israel to the list of parties credibly suspected of sexual violence in conflict zones, drawing Israeli rejection and diplomatic tensions.

headlines.headline: Ebola Cases Nearly Double in DR Congo as WHO Chief Visits Outbreak Epicenter
headlines.summary: The WHO Director-General arrived in Bunia as confirmed Ebola cases doubled within days, with the outbreak spreading amid insecurity and distrust. Aid cuts are hampering response efforts in the conflict-affected region.

perspectives.angle: Ebola death rate 30-50% as WHO chief arrives in DRC outbreak zone
perspectives.summary: The World Health Organization estimates the death rate from the Ebola outbreak in Democratic Republic of Congo at 30-50%, with at least 240 suspected deaths. WHO chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus arrived to support containment efforts.

headlines.headline: Israeli Soldiers Reach Nabatieh, One of Southern Lebanons Biggest Cities
headlines.summary: Israeli ground forces have advanced into Nabatieh, one of the largest cities in southern Lebanon, marking a significant escalation in the Israel-Hezbollah conflict as military talks between the sides begin.

headlines.headline: Iran Accuses Trump of Betraying Diplomacy as Hormuz Blockade Continues
headlines.summary: An adviser to Iran Supreme Leader warned that Trump is betraying diplomacy for the third time by maintaining the naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz, demanding authorization for all commercial vessels transiting the strategic waterway.

headlines.headline: Russia Recalls Ambassador to Armenia Over EU Pivot
headlines.summary: Russia recalled its ambassador from Armenia a day after Putin warned Yerevan against seeking EU membership, referencing the Ukrainian scenario. Armenia has been pivoting away from Russia toward the West amid regional tensions.

headlines.headline: Britain Signs Underwater Drone Deal with US and Australia
headlines.summary: The UK, Australia and US signed an agreement to develop cutting-edge underwater military drone technology under AUKUS, expanding defense cooperation amid growing concerns about Chinese naval activity in the Indo-Pacific.

headlines.headline: India Signs BrahMos Missile Deal with Vietnam, Nears Pact with Indonesia
headlines.summary: India signed a defense agreement with Vietnam for BrahMos missiles and is close to finalizing a similar deal with Indonesia, as New Delhi deepens military partnerships with ASEAN nations amid regional tensions.

headlines.headline: Nigeria Puts 21 States on Ebola Alert as Regional Outbreak Spreads
headlines.summary: Nigerian health authorities placed 21 states on alert for Ebola virus importation as neighboring DRC battles a growing outbreak. The WHO confirmed cases have nearly doubled in days, with regional spread concerns mounting.

headlines.headline: US Defense Secretary Hegseth Tones Down China Rhetoric at Singapore Forum
headlines.summary: At the Shangri-La defense forum, Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth assured Pacific allies of US commitment to the region while softening previous warnings about China, following Trump’s recent Beijing summit with Xi Jinping.

headlines.headline: US Military Blocks Sixth Ship in Iranian Port Blockade
headlines.summary: A Gambia-flagged bulk carrier became the sixth vessel stopped by US forces attempting to breach the blockade of Iranian ports. The fragile ceasefire continues as nuclear negotiations proceed.

headlines.headline: Philippines Warns of Ongoing China Threat Despite Trump-Xi Summit
headlines.summary: Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro Jr. stated the Philippines remains under “severe threat” from China despite recent US-China diplomatic thaw, highlighting ongoing regional tensions.

headlines.headline: US Plans Accelerated Troop Withdrawal from Europe
headlines.summary: The Pentagon plans to speed up the withdrawal of US troops from European bases and will present proposals to NATO allies next month, according to German newspaper Welt am Sonntag.

headlines.headline: IMF, World Bank, IEA Chiefs Warn of Summer Fuel Scarcity from Hormuz Crisis
headlines.summary: Heads of major international economic institutions warned that prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz could cause severe fuel shortages during peak summer demand, straining global energy supplies.

headlines.headline: US General holds rare meeting with Cuban military officials near Guantanamo Bay
headlines.summary: The head of US Southern Command met with Cuban military officials for the first time in recent memory amid growing concerns in Cuba about possible US military action.

headlines.headline: China creates autonomous drone warfare algorithm for jamming environments
headlines.summary: Chinese scientists developed a smart web algorithm that identifies objects as friend, foe or terrain, enabling drone swarms to operate when communication is jammed and vision is blocked.

headlines.headline: 17 nations launch pact to protect undersea cables without US and China
headlines.summary: Singapore announced a new pact with 17 nations to collaborate on protecting vital undersea cables, with both the US and China notably absent from the agreement.

headlines.headline: US softens tone on China at Shangri-La Dialogue after Xi-Trump summit
headlines.summary: Defense Secretary Hegseth avoided directly describing Beijing as a threat at the regional security forum, in contrast to previous years, following the recent Trump-Xi summit.

headlines.headline: US, UK and Australia to Develop Underwater Drone Technology Under AUKUS Pact
headlines.summary: The three nations announced plans to jointly develop underwater drone technology to protect undersea cables and boost naval defense capabilities. The initiative is part of the AUKUS military partnership aimed at countering China's growing influence in the Indo-Pacific region.

headlines.headline: Lula Slams US Designation of Brazilian Gangs as Terrorist Organizations
headlines.summary: Brazil's President Lula da Silva said Brazil will not be treated as a 'tinpot country' after the US designated the PCC and Red Command cartels as foreign terrorist organizations. The move is seen as a boost for Lula's far-right challenger Flavio Bolsonaro in October's presidential election.

headlines.headline: EU Set to Release 16 Billion Euros to Hungary After Orban's Defeat
headlines.summary: The European Union is preparing to release 16 billion euros in previously withheld funds to Hungary after the defeat of Viktor Orban's government. The funds were frozen due to rule of law and anti-corruption concerns, and will now be released as Hungary enacts required reforms.

headlines.headline: Mexico Senate Passes Bill Allowing Election Annulment for Foreign Interference
headlines.summary: Mexico's senate approved a constitutional amendment that would allow election results to be annulled for 'foreign interference,' defined to include illicit financing, propaganda, misinformation and digital manipulation. Critics say the bill could give the ruling party power to overturn opposition victories.

headlines.headline: Iranian Hard-Liners Mobilize to Derail Potential US Peace Deal
headlines.summary: A political fight is unfolding in Iran as hard-liners use rallies, state media and public statements to undermine negotiations with the US. The faction opposes concessions to Washington as Trump deliberates on a framework agreement to end the conflict.

headlines.headline: Italy cancels Kanye West and Travis Scott concerts over security concerns
headlines.summary: Italian authorities banned two concerts in Reggio Emilia due to public order concerns and potential protests. West has faced cancellations across Europe following antisemitic remarks, while Scott has been under scrutiny since the Astroworld crowd crush that killed 10 people.

headlines.headline: Truck crash kills 22 Afghan refugees returning from Pakistan
headlines.summary: A truck carrying Afghan refugees returning from Pakistan overturned in eastern Afghanistan, killing at least 22 people including 10 children. The crash comes as millions of Afghans have returned from Pakistan and Iran following crackdowns on migrants.

headlines.headline: Israeli troops push deeper into Lebanon as Pentagon talks begin
headlines.summary: Israeli forces entered the southern Lebanese village of Dibbine near Marjayoun as Lebanese and Israeli military officials held their first direct talks at the Pentagon in decades. The violence continues despite a nominal ceasefire that began in April.

headlines.headline: Spain legalizes over 500,000 undocumented immigrants in regularization program
headlines.summary: Spain is processing over 549,000 applications to regularize undocumented immigrants by June 30, a stark contrast to US deportation policies. Prime Minister Sánchez called it an act of normalization for people already part of Spanish daily life.

headlines.headline: US Defense Secretary Hegseth: US Not Turning Back on Asia Allies, But Expects Defense Boost
headlines.summary: At a Singapore security summit, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth reaffirmed US commitment to Asian allies while pressing them to increase their own defense spending and arms purchases.

perspectives.angle: US Defense Secretary Hegseth Tells Asian Allies to Do More for US Support
perspectives.summary: At Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore, Pete Hegseth signaled US commitment to Asia remains strong but expects allies to increase defense spending and capability investments to get priority access to American weapons.

headlines.headline: Hegseth tells Asian allies to boost defence spending for US support
headlines.summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Asian partners must do more to get more from Washington, telling the Shangri-La Dialogue that countries wanting US assistance should increase their own defense spending.

headlines.headline: Germany deploys troops to Lithuania in historic NATO defense move
headlines.summary: Berlin has deployed forces to protect the Baltic country amid fears of increased Russian aggression in Europe. German troops returned to Lithuania 80 years after Nazi occupation, this time welcomed as defenders.

perspectives.angle: German Troops Return to Lithuania as NATO Shield Against Russia
perspectives.summary: Germany has deployed forces to protect Lithuania amid fears of increased Russian aggression, marking a historic return of German troops to a country they once occupied. This time, they were welcomed.

headlines.headline: Japan purchases $14 million in military equipment for Ukraine under NATO program
headlines.summary: Japan contributed non-lethal aid to Ukraine through the Prioritized Ukraine Requirements List initiative, marking a significant expansion of Tokyo's support for Kyiv amid the ongoing conflict with Russia.

perspectives.angle: Japan buys $14 million in military equipment for Ukraine under NATO program
perspectives.summary: Japan contributes non-lethal military aid to Ukraine through the NATO Prioritized Ukraine Requirements List initiative, expanding Asian support for Kyiv.

headlines.headline: Turkey sees strong potential for drone development cooperation with Japan
headlines.summary: Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan discussed expanding defense cooperation with Japan in an interview with Nikkei Asia, also noting that a US-Iran deal appeared "closer than ever" amid ongoing nuclear negotiations.

headlines.headline: Pentagon's $4B SpaceX deal accelerates plans for global aircraft tracking from orbit
headlines.summary: A new satellite network to track aircraft from space could offer unprecedented global surveillance capability and potentially make airborne early warning aircraft redundant, representing a major shift in military air defense architecture.

perspectives.angle: Pentagon Accelerates Aircraft Tracking From Orbit With $4B SpaceX Deal
perspectives.summary: A new satellite network to track aircraft globally could make AEW&C aircraft redundant and offer unprecedented surveillance capabilities.

headlines.headline: Ghana Passes Anti-LGBTQ+ Bill Criminalizing Same-Sex Relationships
headlines.summary: Ghana parliament approved legislation imposing jail terms for same-sex acts and for identifying as LGBTQ+, joining other African nations with restrictive laws amid Western criticism.

perspectives.angle: Ghana Parliament Passes Bill Criminalizing LGBTQ+ Identity
perspectives.summary: Ghana parliament approved legislation punishing same-sex acts with up to 3 years in prison. Bill introduces duty to report prohibited acts to police, awaiting presidential ratification.

headlines.headline: Kenyan Court Blocks US Plan for Ebola Quarantine Facility
headlines.summary: A Kenyan court suspended construction of a US military-built facility designed to isolate Americans exposed to Ebola, amid local opposition and concerns about hosting foreign disease containment operations.

headlines.headline: Zelensky warns Russia preparing new large-scale attack on Ukraine
headlines.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky stated on May 29 that Ukrainian intelligence has information indicating Russia is preparing a new large-scale attack on Ukraine, amid ongoing ceasefire negotiations in the Iran-US conflict.

headlines.headline: Chinese scientists develop algorithm for autonomous drone swarm warfare
headlines.summary: Chinese researchers have published a paper describing an algorithm capable of achieving 100% kill rates in drone warfare by autonomously identifying targets as friend, foe, or terrain even when communications are jammed.

headlines.headline: Trump weighs Iran ceasefire deal as Tehran insists no final agreement reached
headlines.summary: President Trump held a White House Situation Room meeting with advisers on extending the Iran ceasefire but has not made a final decision. Iran says the deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and extend the truce has not been finalized, demanding actions not words from Washington.

perspectives.angle: Trump Concludes Iran Meeting Without Announcing Deal
perspectives.summary: President Trump ended a highly anticipated meeting on potential Iran negotiations without announcing any decision, leaving the Middle East in suspense about the future of the ceasefire and potential military actions.

headlines.headline: Ukraine uses AI-enabled drones to strike Russian supply convoys deep behind front lines
headlines.summary: Ukrainian forces are deploying AI-powered Hornet drones to attack Russian logistics vehicles up to 100km from the front, forcing Russia to shorten convoys. The technology marks a shift in the war's dynamics, allowing Ukraine to target critical supply routes with greater precision.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine destroys two Russian Tu-142 bombers and Iskander system in Taganrog strike
perspectives.summary: Ukrainian drones targeted a Russian military airfield in Taganrog, Rostov Oblast, destroying two Tu-142 long-range naval reconnaissance aircraft and an Iskander missile system, according to Ukraine's Unmanned Systems Forces commander.

headlines.headline: Blue Origin New Glenn rocket explodes during test firing at Cape Canaveral
headlines.summary: Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin suffered a major setback when its New Glenn rocket exploded during a static test fire, destroying part of the launch pad. The explosion creates uncertainty for NASA's Artemis moon program, which relies on Blue Origin landers for upcoming lunar missions.

perspectives.angle: Blue Origin Rocket Explodes During Test, Casting Doubt on NASA Moon Plans
perspectives.summary: A Blue Origin New Glenn rocket exploded on the launch pad at Cape Canaveral during testing, creating a major setback for both the company and NASA Artemis Moon program which relies on Blue Origin vehicles.

headlines.headline: Rwanda signs nuclear cooperation agreement with Russia amid shifting Africa alliances
headlines.summary: Rwanda signed a memorandum of understanding with Russia covering nuclear medicine, research, and potential small modular reactors. The deal reflects Moscow's growing influence in Africa as Western powers face questions about reliability as partners.

perspectives.angle: Rwanda Signs Nuclear Deal With Russia as Africa Shifts Power Balance
perspectives.summary: Rwanda has turned to Russia for nuclear training and research, advancing its ambitions in technology, healthcare and diplomacy while underscoring Africa changing geopolitical alignments.

headlines.headline: Chinese scientists create autonomous drone swarm algorithm for combat
headlines.summary: New AI algorithm enables drone swarms to identify and engage targets independently even when communications are jammed, advancing Chinas military drone capabilities.

headlines.headline: China building launch pads near nuclear missile silos
headlines.summary: Satellite imagery reveals Beijing constructing military infrastructure near its nuclear silo fields, raising concerns about Chinas nuclear force expansion.

headlines.headline: Poland threatens to strip Zelenskyy of top state honour over WWII unit naming
headlines.summary: Poland's President Nawrocki proposed revoking Zelenskyy's Order of the White Eagle after Ukraine named a military unit after the UPA, which was involved in the Volhynia massacres that killed 100,000 Poles. The move strains Poland-Ukraine relations.

headlines.headline: Hegseth Reassures Asia Allies on US Commitment Despite Iran War
headlines.summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth told the Shangri-La Dialogue that the US is not turning its back on Asia while fulfilling global obligations, calling for allies to spend 3.5% of GDP on defense while avoiding needless confrontation with China.

headlines.headline: Canada Strengthens Chinese Trade Ties Amid US Trade Rifts
headlines.summary: Canada is seeking economic diversification and strengthening trade ties with China amid growing trade tensions with the United States, as Alberta separatists gain momentum in what could be Canadas biggest unity crisis in decades.

headlines.headline: Hegseth Calls for Stable Equilibrium Against China Hegemony at Shangri-La
headlines.summary: The US Defense Secretary emphasized Taiwan arms sales remain decoupled from Iran war decisions, outlining America strategic posture in the Indo-Pacific.

headlines.headline: Vietnam Leader To Lam Stresses Balance Amid Rising Pressure at Shangri-La
headlines.summary: Vietnam emphasized its balanced diplomatic approach at the Shangri-La Dialogue as regional tensions increase between major powers.

headlines.headline: Australian MQ-28 Ghost Bat Drone Now Flying Over Pacific From US Navy Base
headlines.summary: Boeing tests of the Australian-born MQ-28 off California demonstrate maturity and promote sales potential to Pentagon and allies.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian Gripen Fighters to Arrive in 2027 With Long-Range Meteor Missiles
headlines.summary: Sweden transfer of Gripens will give Ukraine dispersed operations capability paired with Meteor missiles and radar integration.

headlines.headline: Japan Immigration Crackdown Hits Foreign Curry Shop Owners
headlines.summary: Under Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, new visa rules are forcing some foreign business owners who have put down roots in Japan to leave, affecting Sri Lankan and other immigrant communities.

headlines.headline: Trump Boat Strikes Fail to Curb Cocaine Flow to US, Experts Say
headlines.summary: Despite rising body counts off the South American coast from Trump boat strikes policy, researchers say cocaine remains as easily available in many parts of the United States as before the strikes began.

headlines.headline: Putin Preparing Major Escalation Against Kyiv as Russian Forces Stalled in Ukraine
headlines.summary: Russia warns of systematic missile strikes on Kyiv as battlefield gains stall. Putin seeks to shift domestic war narrative with aerial attacks while Ukraine expands successful long-range drone strikes on Russian energy facilities.

perspectives.angle: Putin Signals Escalation as Russian Forces Stall in Ukraine
perspectives.summary: With battlefield advances grinding to a halt and war fatigue growing at home, Putin appears ready to escalate aerial attacks on Kyiv. Russian losses have nearly tripled compared to last year, and Ukraine has retaken territory around Dnipropetrovsk.

headlines.headline: WhatsApp Messages Undermine Netanyahu Mossad Chief Pick
headlines.summary: Private WhatsApp correspondence reportedly undermines the alibi of Netanyahu controversial pick for Mossad chief, raising questions about the appointment process.

headlines.headline: China Building Launch Pads Near Nuclear Missile Silos
headlines.summary: Satellite imagery reveals China is constructing launch pads near its nuclear missile silos, raising concerns about expansion of nuclear capabilities.

perspectives.angle: China Building Launch Pads Near Its Nuclear Missile Silos
perspectives.summary: China nuclear missiles can reach any U.S. city. New satellite images show Beijing is building a sprawling web of military sites near its nuclear silos.

headlines.headline: Trump ICE Blitz Cost 668,000 Jobs Creating Chilling Effect
headlines.summary: A new report estimates Trump immigration crackdown in US cities destroyed 668,000 jobs, causing labor shortages and forcing companies to reduce operations.

headlines.headline: Ex-California Mayor Pleads Guilty to Acting as China Agent
headlines.summary: Former Arcadia, California mayor Eileen Wang pleaded guilty to acting as an illegal agent for the Chinese government, faces up to 10 years in prison.

headlines.headline: Russian Drone Hits Romanian Apartment Building, Injuring Two
headlines.summary: A Russian drone targeting Ukraine struck a residential building in Romania, injuring two people. Romania says the drone was likely hit over Ukraine by its air defenses.

perspectives.angle: Russian Drone Strike Hits Romanian Apartment Building
perspectives.summary: A Russian Geran-2 drone crossed into Romanian airspace and struck an apartment building in Galati, damaging the roof and sparking fires. Residents describe growing fear as the Ukraine war spills across borders.

perspectives.angle: NATO Condemns Russia After Drone Hits Romanian Residential Building
perspectives.summary: A Russian drone likely hit over Ukraine by air defenses crashed into a residential block in Romania, marking a significant escalation as Russian attacks on NATO territory continue. NATO and EU both condemned the incident.

headlines.headline: Putin Ready to Escalate Ukraine War Amid Stalemate and Domestic Discontent
headlines.summary: With a stalemate on the battlefield and growing discontent at home, Putin appears ready to escalate the war in Ukraine, according to AP analysis.

headlines.headline: Spain PM Fights for Political Survival Amid Wave of Scandals
headlines.summary: Spanish Prime Minister Sánchez faces corruption probes targeting colleagues and relatives, putting his eight-year premiership at risk.

headlines.headline: Trump Declares He Is Lifting Naval Blockade on Iran
headlines.summary: President Trump announced he is lifting the naval blockade on Iran, though the rationale without a signed deal remains unclear and Iranian media is pushing back against his claims.

headlines.headline: Russias Suspected Spy Church Near Swedish Airport Raises Alarm
headlines.summary: Swedish intelligence services warn that Moscow is likely using a Russian Orthodox church near Vasteras airport as a platform for espionage and hostile hybrid activities threatening Swedens national security.

headlines.headline: Macron Offers Truth Commission on Haitis Independence Ransom
headlines.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron announced a joint commission of French and Haitian historians to investigate the legacy of the crippling indemnity France extracted from Haiti for its independence, though he made no mention of Haitian demands for reparations.

headlines.headline: Russian Combat Losses in Ukraine Nearly Triple This Year
headlines.summary: Ukrainian officials report 145,000 Russian casualties in 2026, with losses of 179 per square kilometer of advance—far exceeding replacement capacity. Russia has also sold 27.9 tonnes of gold reserves to fund its war effort.

perspectives.angle: Nearly 500,000 Russian soldiers killed in Ukraine, says top UK intel chief
perspectives.summary: A senior British intelligence official confirmed the staggering casualty figure, reinforcing the consensus that Russia is on the defensive for the first time since 2022.

headlines.headline: Spanish PM Sánchez Faces Political Crisis After Eight Years in Power
headlines.summary: Pedro Sánchez marks eight years as prime minister amid a wave of corruption investigations targeting allies and family members. The opposition has called for his resignation, though he insists he will complete his term.

headlines.headline: EU to release billions in frozen funds for Hungary amid reforms
headlines.summary: The EU agreed to release billions in frozen funds after Hungary's new government under PM Peter Magyar implemented reforms. Magyar called it a "historic" breakthrough.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Strikes Oil Infrastructure and Tanker in Western Russia and Occupied Crimea
headlines.summary: Ukrainian forces carried out coordinated strikes on oil infrastructure across Russian and Russian-held territories overnight May 30, according to Russian Telegram channels. The attacks targeted fuel storage and transport facilities.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine Strikes Oil Infrastructure in Western Russia and Occupied Crimea
perspectives.summary: Ukrainian forces reportedly struck oil infrastructure targets across multiple locations overnight, including in western Russia and Russian-occupied Crimea, according to Russian Telegram channels.

headlines.headline: Zelensky's Decision to Honor WWII-Era Ukrainian Insurgent Army Sparks Outrage in Poland
headlines.summary: A presidential decree naming a Ukrainian military unit after the WWII-era Ukrainian Insurgent Army (UPA) has drawn sharp criticism from Poland, highlighting ongoing historical tensions between the two allies.

headlines.headline: EU Agrees on Tougher China Trade Policy as Beijing Vows Retaliation
headlines.summary: The European Union's six largest economies have agreed on stronger trade defense measures against China, with Brussels warning of a new "China shock" to European industries. Beijing has promised countermeasures.

headlines.headline: UN Blacklists Russian Forces Over Sexual Violence Against Ukrainian POWs
headlines.summary: A new UN report adds Russian forces to the sexual violence "blacklist" for crimes against Ukrainian prisoners of war and civilian detainees, documenting nearly 10,000 cases of conflict-related sexual violence worldwide.

perspectives.angle: UN blacklists Russian forces over sexual violence against Ukrainian POWs
perspectives.summary: The United Nations has added Russian forces to a blacklist over documented sexual violence against Ukrainian prisoners of war and civilian detainees, according to a new report.

headlines.headline: UAE Conducted Dozens of Airstrikes on Iran Using US and Israeli Intelligence
headlines.summary: The Wall Street Journal reports that the UAE carried out multiple strikes on Iranian targets, including the Asaluyeh petrochemical complex, using intelligence provided by the US and Israel during the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Lebanese Army Unable to Disarm Hezbollah, Israeli Army Sources Say
headlines.summary: Israeli military officials assess that the Lebanese Armed Forces lack the capability to disarm Hezbollah, raising concerns about the implementation of any ceasefire agreement in Lebanon.
